celebration: 'AND' IS NOW DECODABLE — a-n-d. The connector word that joins two things together — read it yourself, no longer just heard.

songs.title: D-D-Dad
songs.lyrics:
[Intro — whispered]
D! D! D-d-dad!
(d… d… d…)

[Hook]
D! D! D-d-d!
What begins with D?
D-d-doll! D-d-doll!
It's a doll! (a doll!)

[Verse 2]
D! D! D-d-d!
What begins with D?
D-d-dinosaur! D-d-dinosaur!
It's a dinosaur! (a dinosaur!)

[Verse 3]
D! D! D-d-d!
What begins with D?
D-d-drum! D-d-drum!
It's a drum! (a drum!)

[Verse 4]
D! D! D-d-d!
What begins with D?
D-d-door! D-d-door!
It's a door! (a door!)

[Bridge]
A potato?! (NO!)
P-p-potato! Not D!
(d… d… d…)

[Outro]
D-d-doll! D-d-doll!
It's a doll! (a doll!)
D! D! D-d-dad!
songs.sunoStyle: dark trap, 65-72 bpm, heavy 808 bass, sparse hi-hats, deep whisper-rap verses, kids choir chant hook, playful spooky, minimal, clean vocals, nursery trap
songs.sunoNotes: The D sound is a voiced tongue-tap /d/ behind the top teeth — quick and voiced, NEVER 'dee' (letter name) and NEVER 'duh' (schwa). The 'D-d-dad!' chant is a short bounced tap on the beat. Answer line 'It's a ___!' reuses the Week 1 frame.

songs.title: Dad and the Ant
songs.lyrics:
[Intro — whispered]
(dad… and… dad and…)

[Hook]
Dad and Sam! Dad and Sam!
Dad AND Sam!
Dad and the cat! (and the cat!)
Dad and the ant!

[Verse 2]
Sam and the ant! Sam and the ant!
Sam AND the ant!
The cat and the ant! (and the ant!)
All on the mat!

[Bridge]
Dad is sad. (why is Dad sad?)
Dad can not find the ant!
Sam did it! Sam did it!
The ant is on Dad's hat!

[Outro]
Dad and Sam and the cat and the ant.
(AND, AND, AND — you can read it!)
Dad and a POTATO?!
(PO-TA-TO!)
songs.sunoStyle: dark trap, 65-72 bpm, heavy 808 bass, sparse hi-hats, deep whisper-rap verses, kids choir chant hook, playful spooky, minimal, clean vocals, nursery trap
songs.sunoNotes: 🎉 The whole song CELEBRATES the newly-decodable 'and' — the word that joins two things. One frame: '___ and the ___.' The chant piles up 'and, and, and' so the class feels the connector working. Meet Dad (the tall peg-doll man). Kids choir answers the echoes; whisper the outro, then the potato hits loud.

spreads.artNote: Dad's entrance — a tall wooden peg-doll man with a painted brown beard and a green cap, warm and kindly, spotlit. The biggest member of the cast meets the smallest.
Dad standing beside Sam the peg-doll boy, a hand on his shoulder — the new word 'and' joins two people. Read it aloud: and.
Dad crouching to greet the tabby cat, both looking at each other. 'and' joins again.
Dad, enormous, squinting down through his fingers at the tiny ant on his fingertip — scale gag. He can barely see it.
Sam sits with the tiny ant on his knee, the two smallest becoming friends while Dad looks on.
Dad looking down glumly at his empty hand — the ant has gone missing. Gentle worry, not scary.

spreads.text: Sam did it! The ant is on Dad!
spreads.artNote: Sam points up triumphantly — the ant is sitting calmly on the brim of Dad's cap the whole time. Relief and giggles. 'did' anchored.

spreads.text: Dad and Sam and the cat and the ant.
spreads.artNote: The whole cast together on the mat — Dad, Sam, Segina, the cat, the ant — a family portrait. 'and, and, and' strung across the page in gold.
The potato has joined the family line-up beside Dad, deadpan, wearing a tiny green cap to match. The gag noun is left blank — the class SHOUTS 'POTATO' from the picture.

sentences: Dad and Sam sat on the mat.
Dad is sad. Sam did it!
The cat and the ant sat.
extras: 🎉 'AND' celebration card: a-n-d built in the moveable alphabet, then used to join two picture cards ('cat AND ant') — the connector the class can now READ
Moveable alphabet: build d-a-d, a-n-d, s-a-d, d-i-d, m-a-d, d-i-p
Sandpaper letter 'd' + sand tray — voiced tongue-tap /d/ (watch b/d comes later)
Dad wooden peg-doll man added to the cast basket

teacherFocus: 🎉 The connector unlocks: 'and' becomes decodable this week (a-n-d) — the little word that joins two things. Celebrate it: build it in the moveable alphabet, then use it to link two picture cards ('cat AND ant') and let the class READ the join, not just hear it. Teach the voiced tongue-tap /d/, never 'duh'. Blend d-a-d, s-a-d, d-i-d and meet Dad — the tall peg-doll man, the biggest of the cast, whose gentle story with the tiniest ant strings 'and, and, and' across every page.
